title: Two Sum
description:
Given an array of integers `nums` and an integer `target`, return indices of the two numbers such that they add up to `target`.

You may assume that each input would have exactly one solution, and you may not use the same element twice.

examples.explanation: Because nums[0] + nums[1] == 9, we return [0, 1].
Because nums[1] + nums[2] == 6, we return [1, 2].

hints: A really brute force way would be to search for all possible pairs, but that is O(n^2).
Can we use a Hash Map to store the elements we've seen so far to achieve O(n)?

title: Palindrome Number
description: Given an integer `x`, return `true` if `x` is a palindrome, and `false` otherwise.

examples.explanation: 121 reads as 121 from left to right and from right to left.
From left to right, it reads -121. From right to left, it becomes 121-. Therefore it is not a palindrome.

hints: Think about using a math approach.
Optimize for time complexity first, then space.
